# Read the file content
path = '/mnt/data/file-XPXsJE2GqrzLojCg9E8geX-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

import re, json, textwrap

path = '/mnt/data/file-XPXsJE2GqrzLojCg9E8geX-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()

# Find matched bracketed verses
matches = []
for m in re.finditer(r'﴿(.*?)﴾', text, flags=re.DOTALL):
    inner = m.group(1)
    # compute inner start and end indices (absolute)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    # Trim leading/trailing whitespace from inner
    inner_stripped = inner.strip()
    leading_ws_len = len(inner) - len(inner.lstrip())
    trailing_ws_len = len(inner) - len(inner.rstrip())
    start += leading_ws_len
    end -= trailing_ws_len
    matches.append((start, end, text[start:end]))

len(matches), matches[:10]

openings = [m.start() for m in re.finditer('﴿', text)]
closings = [m.start() for m in re.finditer('﴾', text)]
openings, closings[:10], len(openings), len(closings)

pairs = []
for i, o in enumerate(openings):
    # determine next opening boundary
    o_next = openings[i+1] if i+1 < len(openings) else None
    # find the first closing after o
    c_candidates = [c for c in closings if c > o]
    c = c_candidates[0] if c_candidates else None
    if c is not None and (o_next is None or c < o_next):
        # proper pair
        start = o+1
        end = c
        inner = text[start:end].strip()
        # adjust start and end to trimmed
        start += len(text[start:end]) - len(text[start:end].lstrip())
        end -= len(text[start:end]) - len(text[start:end].rstrip())
        pairs.append((start, end, inner))
    else:
        # incomplete; capture until end of line
        start = o+1
        # find newline after o
        nl = text.find('\n', start)
        if nl == -1:
            nl = len(text)
        end = nl
        # trim
        inner = text[start:end].strip()
        start += len(text[start:end]) - len(text[start:end].lstrip())
        end -= len(text[start:end]) - len(text[start:end].rstrip())
        pairs.append((start, end, inner, 'incomplete'))
pairs

# Verify that the indices map to the exact substrings
def verify_spans(text, spans):
    for s,e,t in spans:
        ext = text[s:e]
        print((s,e), ext == t, repr(ext), '->', repr(t))
        
spans = [(301, 339, 'ثُمَّ لَتَرَوْنَّهَا عَيْنَ الْيَقِينِ'),
 (668, 688, 'لو تعلمون علم اليقين'),
 (703, 721, 'لترونها عين اليقين'),
 (737, 757, 'إن هذا لهو حق اليقين'),
 (1335, 1373, 'رَبِّ أَرِنِي كَيْفَ تُحْيِ الْمَوْتَى'),
 (1919, 1927, 'ثُمَّ لَ'),
 (3196, 3301, 'رَبِّ أَرِنِي كَيْفَ تُحْيِ الْمَوْتَى قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِنْ قَالَ بَلَى وَلَكِنْ لِيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِي'),
 (3526, 3567, 'كَلَّا لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ'),
 (3580, 3618, 'ثُمَّ لَتَرَوُنَّهَا عَيْنَ الْيَقِينِ'),
 (3669, 3704, 'إِنَّ هَذَا لَهُوَ حَقُّ الْيَقِينِ'),
 (4220, 4247, 'يَحْسَبُهُ الظَّمْآنُ مَاءً')]

verify_spans(text, spans)
